candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary row exists, but it is not identified as cost of sales / cost of revenues / COR / COGS-related. The selected COGS bucket is only 'Raw Materials and Consumables', and nothing in the excerpt ties this D&A specifically to that bucket.

Operating D&A component row, but not specifically linked to COGS. Also appears to be a component beneath the broader 'Depreciation and write' summary, so using it for COGS would risk misallocation and double counting.
Operating amortization component row, but not specifically linked to COGS. More likely part of the broader operating D&A line than a COGS-specific add-back.

variables.notes: No explicit COGS-specific depreciation/amortisation row is shown. The visible D&A rows appear as separate operating expense lines at a broad operating level, not as cost-of-sales-specific items. To avoid misallocating generic D&A to COGS, do not add any row separately to COGS.

candidates.why: This is the broad operating D&A summary line shown separately alongside 'Other external costs' and 'Personnel costs' and before 'Total operating expenses'. Since earlier XSGA_COMPONENTS intentionally excluded D&A and no SG&A subtotal was selected, this separate same-level D&A line should be added later to the overhead bucket. Prefer the summary over components to avoid double counting.
This looks like a component of the broader 'Depreciation and write' summary row. Because a summary row exists, this should not be chosen separately.
This appears to be another component of the broader 'Depreciation and write' summary row. Because a summary row exists, this should not be chosen separately.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists of 'Other external costs' and 'Personnel costs' only, and the notes explicitly excluded depreciation/amortization from that earlier selection. A separate operating D&A summary row is visible at the same level above total operating expenses, so it should be added separately. Use only the summary row to avoid double counting its components.
